RaceName: Bath And Wiltshire Air Ambulance Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner for Jonathan Portman last September; sent off 10-1 for stable debut at Newmarket 11 days ago and wasn't disgraced despite shaping as if needing the run; dropped 2lb; better expected here.

RaceName: Bath And Wiltshire Air Ambulance Handicap
Spotlight: This 35,000gns breeze-up buy, who didn't make the track at two, showed a bit in the face of adversity at Leicester two starts ago; didn't run so well over C&D latest but a switch to low-grade handicaps should see her in a much better light this evening; David Egan has ridden two winners, one second and two thirds from ten rides for the yard.

RaceName: Bath And Wiltshire Air Ambulance Handicap
Spotlight: Three runs for each of his former yards and managed a win on Lingfield's Polytrack in January (5f); went close for current connections over C&D in May but two subsequent efforts are hard to gloss over.

RaceName: Bath And Wiltshire Air Ambulance Handicap
Spotlight: Two 5f Polytrack wins last winter and confirmed she's no one-trick pony with some good turf runs of late; involved.

RaceName: Bath And Wiltshire Air Ambulance Handicap
Spotlight: Standout performance when beaten less than 2l into sixth in final qualifying run over C&D; down the field in two track and trip handicaps since (gelded/break in between) and can't be supported with confidence.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well beaten on debut at Lingfield in May (7f, turf) but was bang there over a furlong out before weakening into fifth of 11 at Salisbury 17 days ago (6f); that could be a nice race with the third winning since.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 80-1 when failing to beat a rival on debut at Salisbury last month (6f; Theheatison fared much better); can only watch.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Left debut effort behind at Ripon 13 days ago but will be of greater interest once given a mark for nurseries, which will probably be after this run.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Down the field on Windsor debut then managed fourth of 11 at Salisbury; that much improved effort came on soft ground, as opposed to good to firm, but he's probably worth another chance on a sound surface.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 14,000gns yearling; second foal; dam 8.6f AW winner (RPR 72), sister to 6f 2yo Listed winner Rebel Assault; trainer has run two juveniles this year and they were both well beaten; likely best watched.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: A Bengough, P Inglett And Partners
Spotlight: 40,000gns yearling; first foal; dam unraced sister to 7f Listed winner Jash; good yard 0-4 with 2yos this year but market check advised; wears blinkers; is in the sales next week.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £45,000 yearling; ninth foal; sister to Group-placed 6f-1m2f winner Soldier's Gold (including 2yo; RPR 104), half-sister to three winners including Mr McCann (6f-8.5f, including 2yo; 105); dam 1m 2yo winner (105); trainer having a fine year with his 2yos, including a second in the Coventry; one to consider; like Chasingpursuit, she's in the sales next week.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: First foal; dam 6f/7f AW winner (RPR 95), half-sister to couple of nice 2yos; already gelded; in-form trainer had a first-time-out 2yo winner in May which was well supported; drawn widest but watch betting.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 40-1 last of 12 on debut at Windsor in May (6f; Class 2); can't consider.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Seemingly no fluke about ready debut win at Wolverhampton 15 days ago (6f), despite going off at 33-1; trainer has had four first-time-out 2yo winners this year and the other three have all failed to win again since; 7lb penalty might be tough to defy.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: K Shenton, N Bradley 6 & D Cunha
Spotlight: Small step forward at Chester last month but the betting suggested a bigger one would be forthcoming; likely needs extra; one of two twice-raced Dylan Cunha runners.

RaceName: Hot Air Balloon Company Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Returned to Tony Carroll and left a couple of quiet AW runs behind when scoring over C&D (beat She Went Whoosh by nearly 3l) and at Brighton (6f) in May; came up short in hat-trick bid at Windsor on Monday but was out of his comfort zone early over the bare 5f before plugging on into fifth; not dismissed if turned out quickly.

RaceName: Hot Air Balloon Company Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Landed this race off 7lb higher last year; couldn't get involved over the bare 5f here 18 days ago but his two previous runs over this slightly longer trip were encouraging; likely there or thereabouts.

RaceName: Hot Air Balloon Company Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Authoritative win over C&D on first start for Chris Gordon last May and plenty went wrong when bidding to win that race again two starts ago (surely would have been heavily involved with a clear run); beaten into second at even-money on AW next time but the winner rather stole it; shortlisted.

RaceName: Hot Air Balloon Company Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Won seven times here; back with Grace Harris last two starts; lasted pretty long in front over C&D but was then beaten a fair way out when ridden the same way over the bare 5f here 18 days ago (just held off reopposing stablemate Sisters In The Sky for eighth).

RaceName: Hot Air Balloon Company Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has won on turf but is happiest on AW; expected to be caught out by this shorter trip on his return from nearly five months off.

RaceName: Hot Air Balloon Company Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Best of the rest behind Neptune Legend over C&D in May (sole course run) but she's struggled since.

RaceName: Hot Air Balloon Company Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
TrainerName: Dr Richard Newland & Jamie Insole right
Spotlight: First visit here was a winning one (this trip; did it nicely; May 2025) and he hasn't run a bad race at the track since, most recent example when not getting the rub of the green in a 0-70 two starts ago (finished fifth); soft ground a valid excuse latest; of interest in first-time blinkers.

RaceName: Hot Air Balloon Company Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Triple winner (one C&D) for Ollie Sangster last year; uncompetitive for new connections, including in first-time tongue-tie (retained) at Nottingham four weeks ago; better options elsewhere.
All nine wins achieved here but seemingly wants further than 1m these days; went backwards from a pleasing return last time; goes without headgear for the first time in over five years.

0-19 but found just the even-money favourite who has won again since too strong in first-time blinkers (retained) at Ffos Las last week; should cope with dropping back to 1m from 1m2f and is drawn to attack.
This veteran hasn't tasted success for nearly three years; he ran okay over C&D two starts ago and when sixth at Ffos Las last week but more is needed.
Bitterly disappointing given the promise he showed on debut at two but could pick up a small race; pulled his chance away latest and dropping back in trip looks a wise move.
April's C&D handicap win reads well; not in the same form since but returning to classified company, where she's 1-2, should help.
Best RPR for Adrian Wintle when runner-up in C&D handicap last July; more miss than hit on AW of late; chance if David Egan, who gets aboard for the first time, can settle her.

OwnerName: Miss Jane Edgar & D V Gardner
Spotlight: Sole win came over C&D under Millie Wonnacott last September; had the odd ground/trip excuse since and was messed around in the closing stages at Chepstow last time; chance.
Latest effort, which was her second for this yard/off a break, wasn't bad and gave hope for this new trip of 1m; there are worse options.
Last or second last all four starts for this yard following layoff; sneaks into this lowly 0-50 classified contest but is hard to fancy.
Form figures in classified company read 35124323 but he might be tapped for toe back at 1m this evening.
Peak RPR of 31 in six starts from 5.7f-1m; can't consider; wears first-time headgear (cheekpieces).
Yet to make the frame in 13 attempts and was again beaten a long way at Chepstow on Saturday.

Back-to-back wins over this trip last summer; mostly ran with credit of late, including when lacking a change of gear after travelling well over 7f at Newbury eight days ago; runner-up sole visit here.

Blinkers fitted for both starts in 3yo only handicaps; went down narrowly at Salisbury (7f) then powered home to score readily over C&D; highly likely has more to offer and can defy a 7lb rise taking on mostly older horses.

OwnerName: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore
Spotlight: Scored readily off this mark/under Ashley Lewis last October (1m, AW); turf win came over C&D but looks best watched here having ran so poorly in refitted cheekpieces at Salisbury 17 days ago (finished three places behind Sant Alessio).
Comfortably made all over C&D and made most at Wolverhampton (8.6f; also last year); two weak finishes in 2026 and fellow front-runner Post Rider could spoil his chance here.

Won the last time she contested a Class 5 (made all over this trip at Newmarket last October); first taste of slow ground when showing good bit more than on Lingfield reappearance at Goodwood 19 days ago; claims if taking another step forward for all she faces competition for the lead from Sant Alessio.

Bought cheaply by current connections (was trained by Jack Jones) and made the frame when starting out for them over C&D 18 days ago (off a break; wore hood and tongue-tie together for the first time; headgear retained here); this is tougher.
Failed to beat a rival in two starts for this yard; can't consider.

RaceName: Magnum Scaffolding Contracts Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)
OwnerName: W Sweatman, J Summers, R Foden
Spotlight: Big prices and well beaten in three 1m2f events this spring (good to soft to good to firm); gelded last month; should stay this far and he is open to some improvement now handicapping at a modest level.

RaceName: Magnum Scaffolding Contracts Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: On a stiff mark judged on her achievements in maiden/novice events at up to 8.6f this winter but she is bred to leave those efforts well behind now handicapping over middle distances (dam a Group 3 winner); obvious chance.

RaceName: Magnum Scaffolding Contracts Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Didn't live up to market billing in three AW runs (7f-1m) early in the year; gelded since last seen; not bred to need this far but his sire's few middle-distance winners have been out of mares by stamina influences, as is he; interesting to see if his backers return.

RaceName: Magnum Scaffolding Contracts Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: No threat on stable/handicap debut (1m, AW) in May but much more like it when second of ten in a 1m2f handicap at Windsor (good to firm) last month; should stay this extra distance and she's a strong contender despite her 3lb rise.

RaceName: Magnum Scaffolding Contracts Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Won four times on fast ground last summer, two of them over C&D; possible to make excuses for most of his runs since (the latest on ground slower than ideal and after 164 days off) and this race, under optimal conditions, should be a better guide as to just where he stands at present.

RaceName: Magnum Scaffolding Contracts Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Seven-time winner, the latest on AW (1m4f) in February; quiet on her last three starts but she has slipped down the weights as a result.

RaceName: Magnum Scaffolding Contracts Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 7f win for Ed Walker as a 2yo; modest form on Flat and over hurdles for new yard this year; Royal Ascot was fanciful latest and although this is more realistic, others still bring more compelling claims.

Encouraging stable debut when second of 12 at Lingfield (1m, AW) hasn't been built on, although latest 8l defeat when fourth of 14 at Redcar (7f) came when the first two home raced alone; from the family of an Oaks winner so there's hope on pedigree that this new trip might help.
Lingfield AW classified winner in March but was safely held in two turf outings (also 1m2f) last month; others hold more pressing claims.

Fine record here, with his four runs yielding two wins and a second for Adam West in 2024; followed that trainer to France last year but he was back to being an Epsom resident when close third on stable debut at Lingfield (AW) in April and secured first win for this yard at Windsor (also 1m2f, good to firm) one month ago; 4lb rise but he's won off higher; player.

Strong in the market when he made a winning stable debut at Lingfield (1m4f) and he followed up off 3lb higher at Kempton (1m3f, also AW) three weeks ago; handicapper reacted by dishing out a career-high mark but this locally trained gelding is a C&D winner who needs considering in current mood.

Six visits to Epsom have seen her placed four times, including when second in this race in 2024; narrowly got the better of one of just two rivals when winning at Windsor (11.5f, good to firm) in May and it was a solid fourth in similar event back there a fortnight ago; will probably give her running but more of an each-way prospect.

Four-time winner who made all under Mason Paetel in this race (was 2lb higher) last year for Ed Dunlop; very respectable third on stable debut at Lingfield in May and soft ground may have blunted his finishing effort last time; interesting.

Five-time winner who has been placed in half of his six visits here, including when third in this race in 2024; carries a 5lb penalty for winning at Brighton (1m2f, good to firm) ten days ago but Toby Moore's 7lb claim will aid his bid to go back-to-back; considered.
Good AW effort when beaten a head for this yard in February and it was a fair third at Windsor (also 1m2f) last month; that was just his fourth turf start so maybe he can build on it.
Patchy form across nine starts although latest fifth of 13 at Brighton (1m2f, good) was one of his better efforts; didn't seem to handle the track on his sole visit here, however.

RaceName: Betfred Play Fred's £5 Million EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C and D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: 5-2 and she kept at it when second to a potentially above-average newcomer at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) on her second start a fortnight ago; that was a step up on her debut and it's likely she can do better again; considered.

RaceName: Betfred Play Fred's £5 Million EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C and D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: By first-season sire Blackbeard; 40,000gns foal, £50,000 yearling then 30,000euros 2yo breeze-up purchase; second foal; dam unraced sister to winners Homeland (7f 2yo) and Some Romance (French 11.5f), half-sister to Coventry/Golden Jubilee winner Art Connoisseur; yard going nicely with this year's juveniles and the market should provide some good clues.

RaceName: Betfred Play Fred's £5 Million EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C and D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: Attracted strong initial support before drifting in the market then looking raw and weakening on his debut at Leicester (7f, good) three weeks ago; interesting to see if those early birds come back for a second peck.

RaceName: Betfred Play Fred's £5 Million EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C and D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: 14-1 but he went down by only a neck at Pontefract (6f, good) on his debut at the start of the month; winner and third then had a pop at Royal Ascot, admittedly with no impact, but that was a promising start and pedigree suggests this new trip is the way to go.

RaceName: Betfred Play Fred's £5 Million EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C and D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: Beaten 2.25l for fourth on both starts, latterly at Leicester (7f, good) 18 days ago; distinct frame possibilities again.

RaceName: Betfred Play Fred's £5 Million EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C and D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: 7-1 shot when looking green before fifth of 11 at Salisbury (6f, soft) on his debut three weeks ago; goes up in trip and he's a likely improver.

RaceName: Betfred Play Fred's £5 Million EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C and D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB)
OwnerName: Hold My Beer, N Bradley 6, L Bearman
Spotlight: Moderate form in two starts at 6f on a sound surface but he was punted first time and only 4-1 at Ripon on second start a fortnight ago; something is likely showing up at home that we've yet to see on the racecourse and he can't be ruled out on that score.

RaceName: Betfred Play Fred's £5 Million EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C and D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: 35,000gns yearling; eighth foal; half-brother to winners Goodwood Odyssey (1m-1m4f including 2yo/Czechia; RPR 99), Delta Bay (1m; 84), Coconut Bay (7f/1m2f; 70) and Bay Watching (Greek 6.5f); dam placed 6f/7f (including 2yo; 73), half-sister to 1m Group 3 winner; market check required on debut.

Six-time winner who improved last year and was better than ever when scoring at Catterick (6f, good) on his reappearance nearly three months ago; subsequently absent but unlikely he'd be making the journey down without good reason and may be capable of better yet.

Novice and nursery-winning juvenile (6f, good to firm) who often didn't have things go his way subsequently until he won going away at Kempton (6f, AW) three weeks ago; up 6lb but he's shaped like a smart horse waiting to happen on occasions and maybe that was the start of it.

Back to his best last year and his reappearance second of 13 at Pontefract (5f) in April sent his mark to new heights; hasn't matched that in three starts since but last time's fifth of ten from the widest stall at Chester (6f, also good to soft; not clear run) suggests this mark isn't beyond him; in the picture.

Snapped a losing sequence when down in class to score at Windsor (6f, good to firm) on Saturday; 5lb penalty leaves him with a fair burden to carry but he's in the same grade and should make his presence felt.

Hit the ground running for this yard, his Ripon win in April backed up by arguably unlucky fourth at Goodwood the following month; not beaten far in the Dash here next time and led on the line at Windsor (again 5f, good to firm) last Monday; 4lb penalty to overcome and his best has come at 5f, but he's still considered.

Exposed mare who was 6-44 on turf in Ireland; beaten in claimers on her two starts last month (7f and 1m, turf/AW) so it will be interesting to see how she goes in the market on this first stab at 6f since her only attempt over three years ago; stable debut.

Goodwood 2yo novice winner who kept fairly good company last year; made promising reappearance third (AW) in April and was back in that sort of form when third again at Newmarket (again 6f, good to firm) 11 days ago; was first home of the far-side group that day and the field won't be splitting this time.

Won his first two starts (1m novices on AW) but he's yet to kick on in turf handicaps, initially when fourth of six here (8.5f) in April then fifth of ten at Chester (10.5f) the following month; one of his siblings was a dual 1m4f winner for this yard so maybe the new trip will help.

Yet to win but she's got ability and it wasn't a bad effort when fifth of 12 at Salisbury (1m2f, good) on her return in May; dam is related to St Leger winner Hurricane Lane and Group-class stayer Sweet William so there's plenty of hope this new trip will suit; not discounted on that score.

Unraced at two; showed plenty in her three qualifying runs (all 1m2f on good ground) and was off the mark in four-runner Pontefract maiden when looking better the further she went four weeks ago; pedigree also offers plenty of hope that this step up in trip will bring her on again and her opening mark looks fair; shortlisted.

Secured a fourth win (three on turf) when taking Lingfield handicap (1m2f, AW) on his April reappearance but that hasn't been backed up in two subsequent starts; latest fourth was a step back in the right direction, however, when shaping like this first attempt at 1m4f is worth a go; 1-1 here and he holds each-way prospects.

Won Nottingham handicap (8.5f, good; first-time cheekpieces) for Darryll Holland last year; mostly very consistent for this yard and has placed in three of his four starts (9.5f to 1m6f) this term; handicapper appears to be in charge but maybe first-time blinkers and Billy Loughnane can eke out more.

Just one blip in an otherwise productive campaign since reappearing in handicaps this term, his three wins including head defeat of another improving 3yo at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) 11 days ago; that form looks all the better after the runner-up went in at York on Saturday so this 5lb rise might not stop him; big player.

Caused a 25-1 upset on his debut at Newbury (6f) around a year ago but that remains the highlight and he could only beat one home in both handicaps this term; unraced beyond 1m but does have stayers in the family and his sister won at up to 1m6f.

Multiple Flat/hurdles winner who came back from an absence (after a nasty spill at Plumpton) with encouraging third at Salisbury (good; not clear run) in May; didn't build on that at Goodwood (also 1m6f) this month but he travelled well until soft ground likely got the better of him; drops in trip and he's not ruled out.

Very inconsistent in four starts for this yard this term and his good second at Windsor (11.5f, good) in May was flanked by a pair of 1m2f howlers; mark fine if he's on a going day but sole appearance here (well beaten when favourite last April) raises a further doubt.

Four-time winner (all 1m4f on a sound surface) with three of those in blinkers, latterly at Catterick (good; made all) on his penultimate start; overdid it in the lead before beating one home at Goodwood 12 days ago but could bounce back; cheekpieces (second on all three attempts) are reapplied.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Tackling 1m4f and quick ground for the first time when he bolted up at Brighton on his British/stable debut ten days ago; 5lb extra wouldn't have come close to stopping him that day and he's the standout under a penalty.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Last won back in September 2024 but plenty of creditable efforts since include a third in this race (good to firm) last year; tried this headgear combination when beaten less than 2l at Windsor one month ago and she's got solid each-way prospects again.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: 0-14 but it was a solid third when 50-1 at Windsor (11.5f, good to firm) on his second go for this yard last month; took a massive step backwards when beaten 35l on AW a fortnight ago, however, and he'll need to have his Windsor hat on tonight.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner but he's 0-10 on the Flat and keeps weakening no matter what trip he faces; slipping down the weights but he's difficult to fancy.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Got up on the line to bag 1m4f AW classified event in March but he's made little impact in two subsequent handicaps and is 0-7 here.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Showed next to nothing at 7f and 6f in three qualifying runs last year but her reappearance was much more like it when stepped up sharply in trip and fifth of 13 in Windsor classified (11.5f, good to soft; stayed on in first-time cheekpieces) three weeks ago; still early days ahead of this first handicap.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: AW regular (four wins at around this trip) who was well held in this race last year; not disgraced back on turf when fourth in 1m2f classified event a fortnight ago but that brought his record to 0-8 on grass.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: 2m AW winner but his form gradually tailed off for previous stable; new yard could hardly be in better form but he'd still become of interest only with market support ahead of this reappearance.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Improved effort in first-time cheekpieces (retained) when third of ten at Lingfield (11.5f, good to firm) one month ago; that was just her fifth start and she's one of few legitimate threats to Sarangpur.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Dual winner (11.5f and 1m2f) who was placed in a couple of 1m2f handicaps here in 2024; the wheels have come off subsequently, however, and three starts this year promised little.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Registered an improved effort when chasing home a subsequent winner at Lingfield (11.5f, good to firm; Fletcher seventh) one month ago; could be getting his act together and he needs consideration.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Dual 11.5f Windsor winner last summer and it was a good comeback when second under Billy Loughnane over that C&D one month ago; unraced at two and he's likely got further progress in him this term; leading contender.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Bar one blip he was consistent last term and he clung on to score at Leicester (1m4f, good) last July; nothing wrong with this mark and he ran with credit on last season's reappearance; solid each-way claims.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Went close at Windsor (11.5f) in May and she was winning her fifth race when landing a 1m4f Brighton classified (also good to firm) going away last Monday; 4lb penalty makes things trickier back in a handicap but she's not discounted.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: She had signalled a breakthrough might be coming and it duly did in four-runner event at Nottingham (1m6f, good to firm) on Thursday; can probably cope with this drop back in trip but a 5lb penalty for that nose success does make life harder; still respected.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: This year has been a struggle and his return from a break was a bit of a disaster when 80-1 and tailed off at Yarmouth (soft ground) three weeks ago; has a few questions to answer.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Showed a bit more when dropped into a classified and sixth at Windsor (good to soft) three weeks ago; still fairly early days.

RaceName: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Two runs here came in this race but he couldn't better his 2024 runner-up spot when only sixth last year; reappearance effort wasn't bad eight weeks ago but he's been poor in both starts this month, including when well behind Gearing's Point at Brighton last Monday.

Won a turf claimer in Ireland and landed two AW handicaps (1m/7f) for current connections at the start of 2026; has found life harder since but in some good 3yo only races and latest fifth of ten at Sandown (7f, good) in this headgear combination was creditable; respected taking on his elders.

OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: Made it 3-3 on AW with convincing success over this trip at Southwell in May but a 10lb rise proved more than enough to find him out at York since, taking his turf record to 0-9.

OwnerName: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd
Spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with 6f success at Southwell off 1lb higher; also good third of 11 in 7f Kempton handicap in May and better than eighth of 11 might suggest at Beverley ten days later, fading late after racing too keenly; Mason Paetel has won on him before; runner-up once from two outings over C&D and enters calculations.

OwnerName: P C J Dalby & R D Schuster
Spotlight: Can race freely, just as he did when making all at Haydock (7f, good) last summer; stopped quickly without a tongue-tie on Yarmouth reappearance (gelded) and that aid is back in place now.
Form of 7f Lingfield novice win in January reads well; has flopped in three handicaps since but it's possible a recent gelding operation could help.

Made all over C&D (good to soft) last September on final run for Andrew Balding; failed to build on AW reappearance for new yard when only eighth of 16 over C&D but it was a competitive 0-100 at the Derby meeting and she should find life easier at this level.
Largely in decent nick this season, snapping a losing run at Chester (7f, good to soft) 19 days ago in this refitted headgear combination; retains handicapping scope off 5lb higher.

Quite useful for Eve Johnson Houghton at two, including making all in a C&D maiden (good); not at best in two outings for new yard (same owners) this spring so her hopes are pinned on a return to Epsom sparking a revival.

Won twice for Dylan Cunha, the second a Nottingham handicap (6f) on heavy ground last October when blinkered for the first time; poor efforts all three outings this year, including without headgear for new stable 23 days ago.

OwnerName: Mr And Mrs P Whight And T Clover
Spotlight: Three AW wins at up to 1m for the Crisfords in 2025; well beaten in two 1m handicaps on turf for new yard this year but was better than the result on soft ground at Yarmouth last time, weakening having raced too keenly close to the pace; the return to 7f looks a good move.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Bought for 75,000gns at breeze-up in April; sister to 6f/7f winner Yorkshire (inc 2yo/AW; RPR 104), half-sister to winners My Awele (5f/6f inc AW; 82), Amarachi (6f; 61) and Maritot (French 1m-1m2f); dam unraced half-sister to Italian 7.5f 2yo Listed winner Junia Tepzia; tongue tied for debut but of obvious interest if the market speaks positively.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: St Mark's Basilica filly was bought for 43,000gns as a yearling and improved in value to 80,000euros at a breeze-up in May; half-sister to winners See That Storm (1m/1m2f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 103), Star Chorus (5f/6f inc AW; 92), Bell Song (6f-1m AW; 88) and Bright Summer (7f AW; 81); dam 5f/6f winner (inc 2yo/AW; 93); check market albeit concedes weight to most.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 13.5l behind in seventh at Leopardstown last month (7f, soft, 80-1); others more likely.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Outran 33-1 odds at Cork (6f, good) when third of 14, 2.25l behind Ballinea Star, who took Group 3 honours at the Curragh last weekend; huge player off the back of that despite carrying joint top-weight

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Purchased for 27,000euros as a yearling; half-brother to French 8.5f AW winner Tuuli; dam 7f 2yo/1m2f winner (RPR 91), half-sister to useful winners Tamarillo (1m1f UAE Oaks), Take It To The Max (6f-1m2f) and Wake Up Call (7f), out of 14.7f winner; has the attributes on paper to be a player.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Kodi Bear colt was purchased for 34,000gns as a yearling; dam Listed placed over 5f as a 2yo; okay debut at Roscommon when a 6.5l sixth of ten (7.5f, good), weakening late on; not ruled out but others more appealing

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Didn't run to market expectations when favourite on debut at Cork (5f, soft); better effort at Navan (5f, good) when fourth of nine; this trip can unlock more from her based on her pedigree and she receives weight from a few.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Last of 17 and 26l behind the winner over C&D on debut (good); hard to fancy off the back of that despite the addition of a visor.

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Weak maiden form; produced massive improvement when second at 66-1 over C&D last month on handicap debut; obvious chance now if building on that.

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: 29-race maiden, uneven form this year but two of his better efforts have been over C&D; second in an 18-runner contest in May and third of 16 last month, with I'm Spartacus and Rosato among those behind.

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: C&D second early last month was encouraging; failed to reproduce that form a week later; new headgear combination now.

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: 22-race maiden; satisfactory run over C&D last month; satisfactory run over C&D but was behind Monastere and Livingston Range then

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Did not get the best of runs over 5f at Naas last week; previous fourth over C&D was respectable; needs to reverse form with Monastere.

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Three turf wins, none since a Curragh success in 2024; also a C&D winner during the same campaign; no better than ninth in five outings since April; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Modest maiden form; fair effort on handicap debut when sixth over 7f at this venue in first-time blinkers; led for most of the way when third over 7f at Down Royal 12 days ago; good chance now for in-form stable

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Weak form in maidens; towards the rear at Cork on handicap debut; took a step in the right direction when fifth at Limerick; well supported then; noteworthy.

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-time winner; returned to form when second of 21 over 5f at Naas last week; has tended to show his best form at this distance, though also effective over 5f/7f; holds a definite chance

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Seven wins between turf and AW over 5f-6f for former yard; made a good start for this stable with C&D fourth; midfield last week in a Naas event in which Tawaazon was second.

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Course specialist at Navan, scene of all four wins; mid-division over C&D last time; held by several of these rivals on that evidence.

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Ordinary form in maidens at last autumn; achieved his best finishing position when fifth here last time; that was over 1m2f, and he may find this trip inadequate.

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Best efforts this year have come over 5f on soft ground; stable enjoyed a major sprint win at the Irish Derby meeting on Sunday.

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Nothing of much note in her form in eight starts since a 5f Down Royal win last autumn; potential excuses at Gowran last time, raced too keenly and met some interference.

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: 6-46 on turf; losing run stretches back to October 2024; poor form on AW last winter; shaped reasonably well at Listowel on first run for this stable; ran to roughly the same standard at Limerick; stablemate She's On The Ball is more appealing.

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Six-time AW winner; scored for the first time on turf here on penultimate start; has much the same chance as Rosato on running here just under three weeks ago.

RaceName: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Never in the first six in ten outings between September 2024 and March 2025; best watched on first run for Ado McGuinness.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Rewarded for her consistency in maidens (placed in six of nine) when making a winning handicap debut at Down Royal (7f, soft); appeared to idle when she hit the front and 3lb rise doesn't appear unduly harsh; drops to 6f now and shortlisted

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Best form is on soft, including both wins; won a 5f Navan maiden in October and doubled her tally in a 7f Leopardstown handicap in April; below her best on better ground in two runs since.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Beat 23 rivals when winning a maiden at the Curragh (6f, soft) on her seasonal return in April; well held in Listed contests at Cork and Ayr since; ran well on good to firm ground when finishing ahead of the quality filly Amelia Earhart on career debut last season; in the mix.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: 0-16 but placed in three of her four runs this year; close third over 6f at the Curragh in May (winner won premier handicap last weekend); one place closer at Navan last time when headed in the final strides (5f, good); up 3lb but she's knocking on the door of her first win.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 6f Navan maiden on second start last September and has well at times in strong company since; beaten 5.25l by Sarahmae at Cork (5f, good) and 4.75l behind that level on 12lb better terms at Down Royal last time (5f, soft); others preferred.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 5f Cork maiden last August (good); outclassed in a Longchamp Group 3 later that month; fourth in a conditions race at Dundalk when last seen in September; tries 6f for the first time in handicap bow and worth a market check on seasonal return.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Close third on debut at Galway last August (7.5f, soft); promising effort on handicap debut under a positive ride at Roscommon in May (7.5f, good); eased 1lb and this shorter trip could suit; in the mix.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Bridged a gap of almost three years between wins when landing a C&D claimer (good) in May; did well to take a 2l third of eight at Down Royal (5f, soft) last time after being raised 19lb for her claimer win; in great form and she has a chance if it continues.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Won a Windsor maiden (6f, good) for Eve Johnson-Houghton; never involved for this yard at the Curragh (6f, soft to heavy); better effort over C&D (good) in this headgear when not getting a clear run before running on for third; can do better again off 3lb lower.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice for Kevin Ryan in 2024 but 0-13 for current yard and recent efforts none too persuasive.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 5f AW maiden in February last year and added two 6f turf wins after that, the latest off 1lb lower at the Curragh in August; form tailed off in three subsequent runs; makes seasonal return now with Colin Keane aboard.
Two respectable Dundalk runs; fair effort at Leopardstown on turf debut; mid-division at the Curragh in her first handicap; Colin Keane aboard, but others have stronger credentials
Has made the frame on her last two starts, both over 1m; does not need to improve much to feature here.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Contested two strong Curragh maidens last May/June; returned with never-nearer third of 15 at Cork (6f, soft); another solid display over this trip at Down Royal; makes handicap debut with a fair chance for in-form team.

Runner-up in three soft-ground handicaps from 6f-1m this year before a below-par run on quicker ground; won on yielding ground at Limerick; beaten favourite at Gowran two days later, possibly the run came too soon.
Fair form in maidens last year; disappointing on handicap/seasonal debut at Leopardstown; dropped in trip after a poor run at Roscommon; plenty to prove at this stage.
0-18 record is off-putting despite the encouragement offered by a recent Down Royal second; on the positive side, she has been no worse than sixth in her last five races.
Solid placed efforts in maidens last year for Johnny Murtagh; C&D second in May her best handicap effort so far; first time with cheekpieces.
Handicap mark is based on three runs in quick succession in April/May; not easy to assess her chance, but others preferred.

HorseName: Are You In Or Out right
Spotlight: Gained her second course 6f win on seasonal/stable debut; has a bit to find with Minaun View on Leopardstown running.
Maiden had fair 7f form at two for Tom Cooper; lesser form for this yard, but not disgraced mid-field at Roscommon in May on seasonal debut; could improve.

Tipperary winner last summer was only just denied a second career win over this trip at Killarney in May; had no luck in running at Limerick last time; should not be judged harshly on that; cheekpieces on now.
Bolted up over 6f here last month, beating Monastere who bids to advertise the form in the 5.40 event; should be fine with 7f; 12lb rise is challenging, same rider takes off 7lb again.

Made a low-key start to the season before hitting form last month; second over 1m at Gowran before winning over same course and trip; was carrying a 7lb penalty when fourth over 1m1f at Leopardstown last time.
Improved from first two outings to finish third in a 7f Dundalk maiden last December; was having only her second run in a handicap when third at Gowran last month; open to improvement.

Sprang a 40-1 surprise when getting off the mark at Dundalk in January over 6f; solid Curragh handicap run next time but below that level in two outings last month; fourth of six over 5f at Down Royal on latest.
Enjoyed a successful 2025, winning three times; yet to hit form this term; back on her last winning mark; blinkered for the first time.
Failed to make an impact in three maidens at 7f-1m; upped to 1m4f for her handicap debut at Limerick but it didn't bring about the required improvement; returns to shorter now but others preferred.

One of two for trainer; ended AW campaign winning a 6f nursery in December; returned to Dundalk in March when beaten 0.75l into second over 1m; kept on for fourth back on turf over 7f here last time (good), 2.25l behind Fortuity; down 1lb for that and of definite interest once again.
Never counted after a slow start on handicap debut at Naas (6f, good) and beaten 27l on soft to heavy ground at Leopardstown last time upped to 1m; hard to be enthused by him.

Improved for switch to handicaps and good ground when second at Roscommon (7.5f) and went one better over 7f here next time; claimer negated his rise for the win at Limerick last time, but soft ground a possible excuse for his poor showing; chance back at this track on this ground.
1-14, the win coming over 7.5f at Roscommon in May (good, 5lb lower); middling efforts at the Curragh and Cork since and he's hard to trust on overall form.
Yet to reach the first three; needs to find considerable improvement in first time blinkers; Kc Bear is the more appealing of the trainer's two.
Opening turf form was weak but he found improvement for switch to AW and capped his campaign at Dundalk with a win there over 1m in December; up 6lb for that and return to turf is a doubt.
Dundalk win in January (1m) seems to have been a flash in the pan; turf runs to date offer little encouragement
Not disgraced in her three maidens at 7f/1m, when keeping on from unpromising positions; interesting handicap debutant who is worth a market check.
Never competitive in three maidens over 1m; not much better upped to 1m4f for handicap debut at Limerick (good to yielding); needs to show more.
Weaken maiden form at sprint trips; midfield in a 7.5f claimer before finishing well behind in a Curragh handicap over shorter; good bit to prove, including her stamina over this far.
Very ordinary form in maidens at 1m-1m4f on variety of surfaces at big prices; can only be watched on handicap debut.

Profited from return to soft ground and more positive tactics when making all over 1m at Leopardstown, winning cosily by 5l; couldn't follow up under a 7lb penalty back at Leopardstown a week later when third (winner won again subsequently); that race may have come too soon, but he's 14lb higher compared to his win now and ground doesn't suit.

Best of three maidens came at Navan on debut in October when keeping on for a 5.25l fifth of 15 (1m, yielding); lost a shoe on return at Navan when well beaten (1m2f, heavy, 13-8 fav'); few apparent excuses at Gowran last time; gelded since then and makes h'cap bow; McMonagle prefers Kirkland Sioux.

RaceName: Early Bird Winter Festival Tickets At Fairyhouse.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden from 13 starts but creditable seasonal return at Down Royal (7f, soft) when fourth of 11; could improve with that run under his belt.

RaceName: Early Bird Winter Festival Tickets At Fairyhouse.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Added a second AW win at Dundalk in March (1m); 0-14 on turf but has run some decent runs in defeat; last two runs at Limerick and Roscommon have been satisfactory; back in 0-60 company now; on the shortlist.

RaceName: Early Bird Winter Festival Tickets At Fairyhouse.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Made the breakthrough when landing a Leopardstown 1m handicap in April (good) off 49; ran to a similar level at Cork later in the month when beaten 3l into fourth; disappointing effort over 1m1f at Leopardstown last time when beaten 12.5l, but soft ground a possible excuse; Keane back aboard now.

RaceName: Early Bird Winter Festival Tickets At Fairyhouse.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Has shown glimmers of ability at Dundalk and ran well for a long way back on turf here last time (1m2f, good), prominent turning in before fading; can be expected to improve over that seasonal return with cheekpieces now added.

RaceName: Early Bird Winter Festival Tickets At Fairyhouse.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Won a Cork 1m handicap last season off 40 (good) and added an AW win in September; went close off 2lb higher at Dundalk in April; subsequent two turf runs have been disappointing and his record of 1-21 on this surface is concerning.

RaceName: Early Bird Winter Festival Tickets At Fairyhouse.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to make an impact in nine Flat handicaps; sixth over 1m2f here but tailed off at Down Royal next time; unlikely to be the answer.

RaceName: Early Bird Winter Festival Tickets At Fairyhouse.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Two Flat wins came in 2024; two hurdle runs didn't go to plan but bounced back with a good third over 1m1f back on the level at Leopardstown (good to firm), headed inside the final 110yards; this trip looks ideal for him and a tongue tie is added to the cheekpieces; in the mix off 1lb higher.

RaceName: Early Bird Winter Festival Tickets At Fairyhouse.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Moderate efforts in maidens; tongue-tied for handicap debut but may need this seasonal return.

RaceName: Early Bird Winter Festival Tickets At Fairyhouse.ie Handicap
Spotlight: No win since 2022; finished last in two Dundalk runs back from a long absence and was again last back on turf here in June; impossible to fancy.

RaceName: Early Bird Winter Festival Tickets At Fairyhouse.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Didn't show much in maidens and never in contention in two handicap starts; drop of 3lb further not enough to tempt.

RaceName: Early Bird Winter Festival Tickets At Fairyhouse.ie Handicap
OwnerName: B O'Sullivan/D Whyte/Exors of the Late D McHugh/M O'Loghlen
Spotlight: Winner of a 6f AW maiden in 2024; 0-12 on turf and yet to trouble the judge; one of her better efforts when 6l behind the winner over 1m on soft at Limerick last time; dropped 3lb.

RaceName: Early Bird Winter Festival Tickets At Fairyhouse.ie Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs Annette Coogan & J M O'Reilly & Frank Condra
Spotlight: Weak maiden form; chance is not obvious, perhaps tongue-tie will spark improvement, but others have much stronger claims.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Improved from mid-division Curragh debut (1m2f, good) when second over a slightly shorter trip at Gowran; arguably not sure to stay as well as some of these on pedigree; stable's current good form is a positive.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
Spotlight: Frankel filly is closely related to Group 1 winners Hydrangea, The United States and Hermosa; superbly-bred but has looked limited in four runs; beaten 11l in fifth in Dundalk AW fillies' maiden in April; got closer when taking the same place over 1m4f at Cork in May.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Well-related filly was unfancied on debut in a 1m2f maiden at Leopardstown in April and was well beaten; some allowance should be made for interference suffered around three furlongs down.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: 500,000gns yearling; half-sister by Camelot to a 7f Group 3; dam 5f/6f winner (including three Listed races); dam's side is speedy, but her two races have been over this trip; encouraging Limerick debut in April, took third behind an odds-on stablemate; still looked a bit raw when second (again behind a shorter-priced stablemate) at the Curragh last month.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Showed some ability on recent debut at Leopardstown; plenty of improvement required.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Towards the rear in two 1m2f maidens at Leopardstown; will need a handicap mark to have any chance of winning a race.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Has won only one of her 25 races over hurdles, placed six times, currently rated 95; never close enough to challenge in a 1m7f maiden at Leopardstown 13 days ago on her only Flat start.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Ran green first time out at Dundalk in May; Shane Foley prefers stablemate Altura now.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Last of nine in a Naas maiden also contested by stablemate French Blond; can be ruled out.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Tailed off on debut in a Cork maiden in which Cosmetic finished fifth; stable outsider with Shane Foley on Altura.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Ninth foal; Jim Bolger-bred half-sister by Teofilo to winners Sometimesadiamond (6f inc 2yo/Listed), Level Pitch (7f-1m2f ) and Speiriuil (hurdles); dam a middle-distance winner by Galileo; tongue-tie for debut.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Poor form in bumpers/hurdles; last of ten on AW on her only Flat outing.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Clear of the rest when second to the promising Arapaho Gold on his 5f course debut and made no mistake over 6f at Redcar (good) 16 days later, making all; wandered around under pressure at Redcar but was well on top and should improve again.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Failed to take a step forward from his AW debut when beaten over 6l into third at Hamilton (6f, good) 45 days ago but remains open to progress; hooded first time.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Flashed his tail but stuck on to prevail by 1l in novice event at York (6f, good) on debut; only seventh of 13 when sent off 5-2 favourite for the Woodcote at Epsom (6f, good to soft) 12 days later but likely to have a big say back in calmer waters.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Half-brother to winners Diligent Resdev (6f/7f including 2yo; RPR 92) and Nammos (7f AW/1m turf; 75); best watched on debut unless the betting suggests otherwise.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 80-1, always behind in the Rous seller at York (6f, good) on debut 19 days ago.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: £35,000 breeze-up 2yo who is the third foal of an unraced half-sister to Breeders' Cup Turf Sprint winner Starlust; the stable has yet to have a 2yo winner this season but he still needs monitoring in the betting.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 32,000euros half-sister to winners Feyha (1m2f AW 2yo/1m4f; RPR 71), Martinus (German 7f) and Corazon Blanco (Czech 1m1f); her stable has a couple of useful 2yos this year so she's worth a second look in the betting.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Gelded son of Dream Ahead who went through the ring for 1,000gns as a foal; a watching brief is advised on debut unless the betting strongly hints otherwise.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 4,000euros yearling, resold for 5,000euros; gelding by Arizona; fifth foal; brother to 7f/1m AW winner Glyndwr (including 2yo; RPR 86) and half-brother to two Italian winners; best to look elsewhere on debut unless the market speaks favourably.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Weakened quickly to finish remote last of seven on 7f Wetherby debut 16 days ago.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div I)
OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: Half-brother to five winners, including Third Time Lucky (7f-1m1f; RPR 108) and Baba O'Riley (French 6f-1m including 2yo; 96); would enter the reckoning if the market speaks in his favour.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Pulled 2.5l clear of a next-time-out winner Kodiak Breeze (runs in first division) when overcoming inexperience to make a very promising winning debut over 5f here (good to firm) at the start of May; it's taken a bit of time to get him back to the track but 6f should suit even better and he looks more than good enough to defy a penalty.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Showed pace before fading in well-contested York seller on debut (6f, good to firm) 19 days ago; may last longer this time.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Made some late headway when fourth of seven on Ripon debut (6f, good) a fortnight ago; entitled to improve.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div II)
OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: Space Blues colt who is a half-brother to a couple of very useful winners abroad; needs a betting check for a stable which has had a couple of first-time-out 2yo winners this year.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Finished over 8l behind the reopposing Travellers Girl on her 6f Ripon debut two weeks ago.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 5,000 euros yearling; sister to 1m winner Alabama Calling (RPR 82) and half-sister to four other winners; stable hardly noted for first-time-out juvenile success.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 66-1 and hooded, hinted at ability amidst greenness when fifth of nine on 6f Redcar debut (good) 11 days ago; may do better.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 9,000euros yearling closely related to 7f AW winner River Chorus (RPR 70) and half-brother to winners Sioux Spirit (5f 2yo/6f; 90) and Biloxi Boy (5f 2yo; 76); dam 12.5f winner (including AW; 73); the betting should guide to expectations on debut.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: £1,000 yearling who is the first foal of a mare who was placed 7f-1m (RPR 76); most from the stable are better for a run but the betting should provide more clues.

RaceName: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 28-1 when last of eight on Carlisle debut (5f, good to soft) 22 days ago.

Fairly useful form when placed on first four starts, notably second of nine on 7.5f Beverley handicap debut (good to soft) in May; his run of consistent efforts came to an abrupt halt at Nottingham last time and cheekpieces are reached for on the back of that; shouldn't be far away if back to his best but still likely vulnerable to an improver.

OwnerName: RC Syndicate X and Gary Gillies
Spotlight: His dam was a German 1m Group 3 winner; left summer's AW debut well behind when second to the promising Portcullis in 1m York novice (good) on reappearance in May, keeping on in a style which suggests he's sure to win races.

It took this 87,000euros 2yo breeze-up buy a while to get to the track but he looks capable of making up for lost time after a promising opening run at Carlisle (9f, soft) 22 days ago, finishing well from the rear for a never-nearer third of 12; that doesn't obviously point to this sharper test suiting but he could just be a lot more clued up second time out.

150,000gns yearling; fourth foal; sister to 7f winner Rajala (RPR 75), half-sister to winners Vento D'Estate (Italian 7.5f-8.5f) and Ikigai Star (2m/2m3f hurdle); dam 7.5f 2yo Listed/1m Italian 1,000 Guineas winner (102); tongue tied for debut but Tom Marquand booked and she needs checking out in the betting.
6yo mare who hinted at ability on her 6f Redcar debut in May 2024 but not seen again until trailing home at Carlisle (1m, soft) 23 days ago.
In good hands but no promise when remote sixth over 1m on AW and 7f on good to firm this spring.

Third on both starts nine months apart (gelded in between), actually bettering the form of his debut in a ten-runner race when last of three at Nottingham (extended 1m, soft) three weeks ago; cheekpieces worn there are left off now.
150-1 when tailed off on recent Southwell debut.

Won three sprint handicaps last spring and he is now 9lb lower than for the last of them; 2026 had been a struggle until he emerged from the doldrums with a good second at Ripon (6f, good) two weeks ago; now has something to build on.
6f AW winner as a 2yo but her losing run stands at 15 and this year's efforts don't suggest it will be ending any time soon.

C&D winner last summer; landed a 5f handicap at Catterick off a 2lb higher mark in April but he hasn't come close to replicating it in six runs since; capable if breaking on terms but he carries some risk.
Failed to progress for Hugo Palmer but his two turf runs for this yard in May were both encouraging; edging down the weights and he should appreciate a strong pace at this trip.

OwnerName: The Good Racing Company Ltd & Partner
Spotlight: Ran well on her stable debut at Wolverhampton last October but only run to that level once from four starts this year; returns to sprinting with the tongue-tie discarded; dangerous mark but others appeal more.
6f AW winner for Kevin Ryan; second of ten in a 5f classified event at Southwell on his seasonal/stable debut 13 days ago; no problem with conditions and he's one to take seriously.

OwnerName: John And Tony Jarvis And Partner
Spotlight: Three-time AW winner but 0-14 on turf; his last two runs on grass have offered hope that he can win a small race on fast ground though and he is one for the shortlist.

OwnerName: R Chapman, G J Daly & R J Parks
Spotlight: Won twice last July, including when beating 17 rivals in the corresponding race (good ground; 2lb lower today); arrived in better form last year but she can't be discounted.

Just one win to his name from 21 attempts but he has finished second in two of his three runs this year and is still on a workable mark; new blinkers could spark something extra (ran well in one attempt in cheekpieces) and he's more appealing than many.
Two AW wins over this trip but he wasn't at his best when last seen in March; his three turf starts are all excusable to some degree and he could prove as effective on grass in time.

OwnerName: Hard Held Partnership And Angus Racing
Spotlight: Beaten a nose by Mrs Trump in this race last year when rated 5lb higher; not at his best this year but his Redcar fourth in May offered hope he could add to his 11 wins.
6f AW win in November (visored first time) and placed on a few occasions subsequently; neither start since returning from a four-month break has been near that level though.
She has run some good races in big-field handicaps over C&D and this is her level; not far off her recent best at Carlisle 16 days ago; each-way shout if getting it right at the start.
Regressive 17-race maiden; too much to prove for comfort.

OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: 0-10 but she has snippets of form that give her a squeak; last week's fifth of seven at Catterick wasn't as bad a run as the distance beaten suggests (hampered at a crucial stage) and she's not out of it.

Unplaced all eight starts; got a bit closer to the target when fifth of 12 in a 5f handicap at Newcastle six weeks ago (blinkered first time); suspicion he'll do better at some stage but he is not the obvious answer this time.
Unplaced in all ten starts; whiff of promise at Nottingham in April but he is opposable on balance.
No improvement for handicapping (6f, good and 1m, soft) in May; tongue-tie and cheekpieces added but he has plenty to prove.

OwnerName: Nick Bradley Racing 16 And Jmr Partners
Spotlight: Won a Beverley maiden on her second start but 0-12 since; more realistically handicapped again now and she ran a sound race at Pontefract (6f, good; cheekpieces first time) last month; soft ground excuses latest lesser effort; not discounted down into a 0-70.
Creditable efforts in three Newcastle maidens in February/March (6f twice and 5f); while she still has time to do better, that AW form is not strong and improvement is essential.

Two wins in 6f handicaps in May; beaten favourite in 5f handicaps at York and Thirsk on his last two starts but has left the impression there is still a bit more to come; return to 6f will help and he's a major player.

Off the mark at the 11th attempt when gamely landing a 6f handicap at Ripon (good ground) in April; beaten at short odds when fourth to Simba's Pride at Hamilton shortly afterwards and his form then dipped at Pontefract (5f) four weeks ago; returning to 6f should help; cheekpieces worn last time are quickly discarded.

OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing D Turner B Roberts
Spotlight: Started his 3yo campaign with a 6f win at Chelmsford in March and he followed up at Yarmouth (5f, good) one month later; beaten favourite but performed with credit at Nottingham (5f) and Wolverhampton (6f) since; sneaking up the weights but likely to give another good account.

Promise in his sole 2yo start (6f, good to firm); no progress in two runs this year but he has been sent off a single-figure price on each start and could take a step forward now handicapping at a realistic level.
Off the mark at the eighth attempt with a ready win at Redcar (6f, good) 11 days ago, strong at the finish; up 5lb in a higher grade but he's not fully exposed just yet.

Yet to win but he has finished second three times in novice/maiden company; cheekpieces have been worn for his three handicap runs and he has finished unplaced, albeit a bit of mid-race interference didn't help his cause over C&D four weeks ago; now drops into a 0-70 and could yet come good.
0-10 and she has yet to shine for her new stable, beaten 14l behind Simba's Pride at Ripon five weeks ago; lots to prove.
His two runs this year have both threatened better and this well-bred 3yo moves into handicaps with some potential; he could do with settling better if he is to fulfil it though.

OwnerName: J Raybould N Skinner D Jenkins
Spotlight: The addition of a visor saw him break his duck in a 5f handicap at Newcastle in May; always on the back foot after an awkward start at York 19 days ago; this is weaker and he should be fine back at 6f but others appeal as being better treated.
Front-runner who landed three 1m handicaps last autumn, the last two at Newcastle (Tapeta); twice placed at Kempton (1m3f, Polytrack) earlier this year but recent efforts less encouraging.

Brother to 7f-1m2f winner Al Mubhir (RPR 115); has made steady progress in his three starts, finishing 4l second to a useful prospect in 1m3f Hamilton maiden (good to soft) three weeks ago; makes handicap debut; should continue to improve.
Best effort when third on 1m4f Newmarket handicap debut (good) 12 days ago, staying on in a style which suggests there could be more to come as his stamina is drawn out.
Consistent form when runner-up in all three novice events and third of nine on handicap debut at Chester (1m2f, good to soft); rallied last time as if 1m4f could help.

Failed to progress for a switch to handicaps when fourth of nine at Epsom (1m2f, soft) on Derby day but stuck on in a style which suggests this step up to 1m4f could unlock some improvement; likely to go well.
Dropped into Class 3 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 16 days ago and ran a good race for second, edging left in the closing stages but keeping on well regardless; still has handicapping scope up 1lb.
He has run well over C&D in the past, including in May, and he didn't run badly when third of six in a Class 3 handicap at Pontefract (6f, good) ten days ago; each-way shout.
Got up late to win a 6f handicap at Pontefract on his stable debut in April; no impact in a 7f handicap at Epsom on Oaks day; others bring stronger credentials.
6-21 on AW but still seeking a first turf win after 14 attempts; ran okay at York last month without suggesting he was the answer today.

OwnerName: N Skinner D Jenkins J Glover
Spotlight: Back to form with a bang when seeing off 19 rivals at York (6f, good) in May; similar form when fourth of 21 back at York 18 days ago; should remain competitive.
Well handicapped on 2025 best; ran a fine race for third on his seasonal return at Ripon in April but two heavy defeats have followed (behind Novello Lad latest); needs a full revival.

Won his first two starts in maiden/novice company; second of nine at Windsor (6f, good to firm) on his handicap debut last June but it has been tougher going since; latest third at Ripon was a promising enough return to action though and he should give a good account.

Progressive sprinter in 2025 (following wind surgery) and he has carried on the good work this year, winning 6f handicaps at Doncaster in May (good to soft) and June (good to firm); up 4lb in a stronger race but he may not have finished progressing just yet.

He had dropped a long way in the weights before ending a losing run at Newcastle (5f) in March; added a C&D win in April and he has run well in defeat in both runs since; creeping back up the weights and had a 53-day break but he has the ability to go well.
Fine servant down the years but he hasn't won over 6f since August 2022; on a good mark and can pop up soon but would like to see more from him before getting involved.

RaceName: William Hill Summer Cup Saturday 1st August Book Now Handicap
Spotlight: It took him 17 attempts to put his head in front, finally opening his account in a 6f maiden at Newcastle in March; solid second in a 6f Southwell handicap one month later; absent for 81 days and although he isn't on a bad mark, he does have to prove his stamina for 7f.

RaceName: William Hill Summer Cup Saturday 1st August Book Now Handicap
Spotlight: He has run a string of good races in 2026, winning for the second time when comfortably seeing off five rivals at Beverley (7.4f, good) last week; that effort was backed up by the Topspeed figure and he can't be discounted under a 5lb penalty.

RaceName: William Hill Summer Cup Saturday 1st August Book Now Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 7f handicap at Redcar (good to firm) last summer off 2lb lower; not at her best over 1m this year; cheekpieces discarded today.

RaceName: William Hill Summer Cup Saturday 1st August Book Now Handicap
Spotlight: Low-key run at Newmarket (6f, good) 12 days ago; return to a longer trip is in his favour but others are more appealing for win purposes.

RaceName: William Hill Summer Cup Saturday 1st August Book Now Handicap
Spotlight: Over two years since his sole success and well held in two starts this year; on a dangerous mark but that has been the case for some time; returning to 7f not enough to tempt.

RaceName: William Hill Summer Cup Saturday 1st August Book Now Handicap
Spotlight: Won a maiden over C&D last summer; back to form with a front-running second of seven in a fillies' handicap at Catterick (7f, good to firm; tongue tied first time) nine days ago; on a dangerous mark and one of two solid contenders for David O'Meara.

RaceName: William Hill Summer Cup Saturday 1st August Book Now Handicap
Spotlight: Still to add to her 2yo novice win; finished second of eight at Redcar (7f, good) in May but not in the same form in either run since; opposable.

RaceName: William Hill Summer Cup Saturday 1st August Book Now Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-race maiden; solid effort over 1m at Ayr (soft) 12 days ago but that was a Class 6 and he is facing different conditions here.

RaceName: William Hill Summer Cup Saturday 1st August Book Now Handicap
OwnerName: Buist, Shields, Thompson And Mrs R Carr
Spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden; ran his best race for new connections when staying on over 6f at Doncaster 11 days ago; stays 7f but he may need a drop into Class 6 to break his duck.

RaceName: William Hill Summer Cup Saturday 1st August Book Now Handicap
Spotlight: 7lb below his last winning mark but that was back in October 2024; retains ability and he has been placed in all three C&D runs; should give his running.

RaceName: Perrigo Consultant Tax Saving Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Irish point win in 2025 was followed by two bumper successes in Britain; failed to win over hurdles since though and he hasn't looked about to score over fences of late either; probably needs more weight off his back.

RaceName: Perrigo Consultant Tax Saving Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won maiden hurdle in 2024; best effort for a long time when third of 11 here (2m7f, good; closely matched with Pike Road) in June but he was beaten too far in one previous chase start under rules, at Taunton last November, to have confidence now back over fences.

RaceName: Perrigo Consultant Tax Saving Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Career has had its ups and downs but he won two chases (3m1f and 3m6f) in March 2025 and ran one of his better races of the year when second of five at Stratford (3m4f, good to firm; blinkers replaced cheekpieces) in May; capable of going well over this lesser test if holding his form.

RaceName: Perrigo Consultant Tax Saving Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Successful handicap hurdle debut at Wetherby (about 2m5f, good) last October; no match at all for the winner on chase debut at Bangor (about 2m4f, good) in April but the return to this longer trip can help; first-time visor replaces cheekpieces; yard runs two.

RaceName: Perrigo Consultant Tax Saving Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Consistent either side of two wins at about 2m4f last December (good to soft and heavy) but there's nothing eyecatching about his mark now and he needs to find extra for the new trip.

RaceName: Perrigo Consultant Tax Saving Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has had some modest results since going close on yard debut at Uttoxeter (2m4f, heavy) before Christmas; others much higher up the list.

RaceName: Perrigo Consultant Tax Saving Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Two hurdle wins in 2025, at about 3m on good and soft, and his recent second here was a return to form; has lower chase mark but hard to assess back over fences, having weakened on chase debut then unseated rider three out at Ludlow (still led sole remaining rival) that month; shade to prove.

RaceName: Perrigo Consultant Tax Saving Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Two hurdle wins, most recently in February, but she's been out of luck in three chase runs, including when a remote last of four at Uttoxeter (about 3m, good) in June; has it to prove for now.

RaceName: Perrigo Consultant Tax Saving Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Bumper winner on debut in 2023; finally off the mark over hurdles (2m7f, good) last September but she's run to an ordinary level back in handicaps on last two starts and goes chasing with something to prove.

RaceName: Perrigo Consultant Tax Saving Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Had a spell in the doldrums for previous yard, so was well treated on debut for this one at Hereford (2m5f, good) in May when having a match in safe keeping from a fair way out; that form amounted to little (second well below best then and since) but this one has gone back up just 2lb and should stay this first attempt beyond 2m5f under rules.

RaceName: Perrigo Consultant Tax Saving Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Three chase wins since April 2025, at 2m and 2m4f, but he was pulled up in two of his last three starts for Olly Murphy and has a stamina query over the new trip on first run since January.

RaceName: Brookes Wedding Feast & Fair Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Maiden hurdler who is already doing better over fences, having stayed on well to win at Warwick (3m, good) in June on his second chase start; had run well over a similar trip to today's the time before and, although back up 5lb, he should have more to come.

RaceName: Brookes Wedding Feast & Fair Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Lightly raced; tenacious winner of 2m5f chase at Newton Abbot (good) last June but soft ground may have counted against her next time and she wasn't seen after July; a long way back on her recent yard debut but there's still hope for her at this level on anything like her old form.

RaceName: Brookes Wedding Feast & Fair Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
OwnerName: The Galloping Dentist At 45 The Green
Spotlight: Hurdle winner here (2m7f, good) last August for previous yard but he's had a rocky ride for this one, including on last two chase starts (the latest following wind surgery); hard to be confident of a revival.

RaceName: Brookes Wedding Feast & Fair Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Very modest hurdle form but she went chasing off a career-low mark at Southwell (2m4f, good) in June and took a healthy step forward, keeping on well against one in a good run of form; back up 2lb; can threaten.

RaceName: Brookes Wedding Feast & Fair Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has shown promise in a bumper and over hurdles but he's been pulled up on his last two starts, over hurdles and on chase debut, and returns from his second wind surgery since the autumn with plenty to prove.

RaceName: Brookes Wedding Feast & Fair Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Better known as a pointer, adding two more in the spring, and her challenge didn't last long over C&D three weeks ago; up against it from out of the weights.

RaceName: Brookes Wedding Feast & Fair Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Four chase wins include two here, one in this race last year; creditable return from a break at Fontwell in April but he's struggled in two Warwick runs since, the step up to 3m seemingly not the main issue last time; just below his latest winning mark though so hard to dismiss.

RaceName: Brookes Wedding Feast & Fair Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Looked a potential improver when convincing winner on second chase start, over C&D (good) last June; hasn't delivered on that promise since and was right off her game at Ffos Las in April; at least this is easier.

RaceName: Brookes Wedding Feast & Fair Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Useful chase strike-rate, winning three of six completions (at up to about 2m3f; good to soft and good) since October; well below best over C&D latest but overall form suggests he could soon put that behind him.

RaceName: Worcestershire Ambassadors Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fair bumper winner; yet to add to that over hurdles but she has made the frame on all her six runs, a non-staying fourth in 2m4f Fakenham novice in May on latest; needs considering back in trip for her chasing debut.

RaceName: Worcestershire Ambassadors Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Modest maiden hurdler; yet to offer much in two runs in this sphere, hooded when a well-held third of four in 2m4f handicap here 25 days ago; lots more is required from 2lb out of the handicap.

RaceName: Worcestershire Ambassadors Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
